title: What to Expect in a Partial Government Shutdown
title_cn: 部分政府停摆中会发生什么

paragraphs.cn: 美国东部时间2026年1月31日凌晨12:01更新

paragraphs.en: Funding for large parts of the government lapsed Saturday morning, after Congress missed a midnight deadline. While the Senate passed its funding package late Friday, the House still needs to approve it—but that chamber isn’t expected back until Monday.
paragraphs.cn: 周六上午，政府大部分部门的资金中断，此前国会错过了午夜截止日期。虽然参议院已于周五晚些时候通过了其资金方案，但众议院仍需批准——但预计众议院要到周一才能复会。

paragraphs.en: Unlike last year’s record-setting full shutdown, this shutdown won’t affect all agencies and programs, since Congress already has passed into law funding for some departments. Also, because the lapse in funding is expected to be short in duration, federal workers aren’t expected to miss paychecks. But nothing is certain until the package is sent to President Trump and he signs it into law.
paragraphs.cn: 与去年创纪录的全面停摆不同，这次停摆不会影响所有机构和项目，因为国会已经通过了部分部门的资金法案。此外，由于资金中断预计持续时间较短，联邦工作人员预计不会错过工资。但只有当方案送交特朗普总统并由他签署成为法律后，一切才能确定。

paragraphs.en:

paragraphs.cn: 订阅《华尔街日报》以继续阅读您的文章

vocabulary.example: What to Expect in a Partial Government Shutdown.
vocabulary.example_cn: 部分政府停摆中会发生什么。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A cessation of activity or operation.
vocabulary.example: What to Expect in a Partial Government Shutdown.
vocabulary.example_cn: 部分政府停摆中会发生什么。

vocabulary.meaning_en: (Of a right, privilege, or agreement) no longer valid; expired.
vocabulary.example: Funding for large parts of the government lapsed Saturday morning, after Congress missed a midnight deadline.
vocabulary.example_cn: 周六上午，政府大部分部门的资金中断，此前国会错过了午夜截止日期。

vocabulary.meaning_en: The latest time or date by which something should be completed.
vocabulary.example: Funding for large parts of the government lapsed Saturday morning, after Congress missed a midnight deadline.
vocabulary.example_cn: 周六上午，政府大部分部门的资金中断，此前国会错过了午夜截止日期。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A set of proposals or terms offered or agreed to by a government or organization.
vocabulary.example: While the Senate passed its funding package late Friday, the House still needs to approve it—but that chamber isn’t expected back until Monday.
vocabulary.example_cn: 虽然参议院已于周五晚些时候通过了其资金方案，但众议院仍需批准——但预计众议院要到周一才能复会。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Officially agree to or accept as satisfactory.
vocabulary.example: While the Senate passed its funding package late Friday, the House still needs to approve it—but that chamber isn’t expected back until Monday.
vocabulary.example_cn: 虽然参议院已于周五晚些时候通过了其资金方案，但众议院仍需批准——但预计众议院要到周一才能复会。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A business or organization providing a particular service on behalf of another business, person, or group. (In this context: government departments or organizations)
vocabulary.example: Unlike last year’s record-setting full shutdown, this shutdown won’t affect all agencies and programs, since Congress already has passed into law funding for some departments.
vocabulary.example_cn: 与去年创纪录的全面停摆不同，这次停摆不会影响所有机构和项目，因为国会已经通过了部分部门的资金法案。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A planned series of future events, items, or performances. (In this context: organized sets of activities or services)
vocabulary.example: Unlike last year’s record-setting full shutdown, this shutdown won’t affect all agencies and programs, since Congress already has passed into law funding for some departments.
vocabulary.example_cn: 与去年创纪录的全面停摆不同，这次停摆不会影响所有机构和项目，因为国会已经通过了部分部门的资金法案。

vocabulary.meaning_en: The time during which something continues.
vocabulary.example: Also, because the lapse in funding is expected to be short in duration, federal workers aren’t expected to miss paychecks.
vocabulary.example_cn: 此外，由于资金中断预计持续时间较短，联邦工作人员预计不会错过工资。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A check for salary or wages.
vocabulary.example: Also, because the lapse in funding is expected to be short in duration, federal workers aren’t expected to miss paychecks.
vocabulary.example_cn: 此外，由于资金中断预计持续时间较短，联邦工作人员预计不会错过工资。
